info.name: Wallet Service
info.description:
Core Wallet HTTP API.

**Setup**:
1. Import this collection + `wallet-service.postman_environment.json`.
2. Start the docker stack (`docker compose up -d`) and seed wallets (see `db/seeds/`).
3. Run `make run` in `services/wallet-service/` (binds to `http://localhost:8090`).
4. Select the **Wallet Service — local** environment in Postman.
5. Run requests in the order below to exercise the full flow.

**Conventions**:
- Every transactional request auto-generates a unique `reference` (idempotency key).
- `X-Caller-Subject` → `audit.actor` GUC → `created_by` / `updated_by`.
- `X-Channel` → `audit.channel` GUC → `channel` column.
- `X-Request-Id` correlates one client request across services.

**Success envelope** (every 2xx except `GET /healthz`):
```json
{
  "errorCode": "00000",
  "errorMessage": "Success!",
  "data": { /* business payload: tran_internal_key, event_uuid, new_balance, status, client_no, acct_no, restraint_id, etc. */ },
  "trace_id": "req-xxx",
  "timestamp": "2026-06-05T10:23:45+07:00"
}
```
Business fields live under `data` — Postman tests read them as `body.data.<field>`.

**Error envelope**:
```json
{
  "errorCode": "P0060",
  "errorMessage": "INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS: balance 50000 < required 100000",
  "detail": "balance 50000 < required 100000",
  "iso20022_reason_code": "AM04",
  "transaction_status": "RJCT",
  "trace_id": "req-xxx",
  "timestamp": "2026-06-05T10:23:45+07:00"
}
```
Error-envelope fields (`errorCode`, `errorMessage`, `detail`, `iso20022_reason_code`, `trace_id`, `timestamp`) stay at the top level.

See full error catalog: `docs/specs/error_management.md`

exec: // Generate a UNIQUE idempotency reference for every transactional request.
// Each request gets its own value so we never accidentally hit the SP's
// duplicate path with a cached response from a different operation shape.
// (Tests that explicitly verify idempotency override this in their own
// per-request pre-request script — see 'Idempotency' under Error scenarios.)
// Collection-level baseline: every response must carry a request_id header.

request.description: Create a client master record (identity only; no KYC). global_id uses {{$timestamp}} to stay unique. Captures {{client_no}}.
Update mutable client fields (uses {{client_no}} from the create request).
client_type must be IND or CORP.
Link a bank account to a client. acct_no is plaintext in -> SP encrypts to ACCT_NO_ENC (pgp_sym_encrypt). is_default=true makes it the sole default. Captures {{bank_link_id}}.
Make an existing linked bank the client's sole default (clears any prior default). 404 BANK_LINK_NOT_FOUND if the link_id is not owned by the client.
Masked client + KYC profile (name, CCCD, phone all masked). Run create-client/onboard first to set {{client_no}}.

query.description: keyset cursor: client_no from the previous page's next_cursor

request.description: Masked client list (v_client_masked), keyset-paginated by client_no ascending. Optional status / client_type filters. When a full page is returned, next_cursor is set — pass it as ?after= for the next page (captured to {{client_after}}).

exec: if(pm.response.code===200){ const b=pm.response.json(); pm.test('list shape', ()=>{ pm.expect(b).to.have.property('items'); pm.expect(b.items).to.be.an('array'); pm.expect(b).to.have.property('page_size'); pm.expect(b).to.have.property('count'); }); if(b.next_cursor){ pm.environment.set('client_after', b.next_cursor); } }

request.description: Update KYC (US-1.2): set tier/status/risk + eKYC evidence. Reaching tier>=2 stamps verified_at.

Unmasked client profile (raw name + CCCD), ops/PII path. Requires the PII read pool (DB_PII_DSN -> wallet_pii_ro) to be configured; if it is unset the unmask query returns 500 (the masked GET /v1/clients/:id still works).
All wallets owned by the client (account profiles, no PII). Run create-client/onboard + open-account first to set {{client_no}}.
Client 360 (MASKED): profile + wallets + linked banks (acct_no masked '****'+last4) + restraints. PII masked (wallet_app).

Client 360 (UNMASKED, ops/wallet_pii_ro): raw profile + DECRYPTED phone/email + banks (acct_no decrypted) + restraints. Requires the PII read pool (DB_PII_DSN -> wallet_pii_ro) and role wallet.ops.read when JWT is enabled.

query.description: keyset cursor: client_no from previous next_cursor

request.description: UNMASKED client list (ops/wallet_pii_ro): raw name + CCCD + decrypted phone/email, keyset-paginated. Pair of the masked GET /v1/clients.
Treasury Service credits a wallet (s2s). 201 POSTED on first call, 200 DUPLICATE on retry with same `reference`.
Wallet → wallet in-book transfer with fee + VAT. Fee config from `WLT_TRAN_DEF.TRFOUT` (5,500 VND fixed, 10% VAT inclusive = 5,000 net + 500 VAT).
Internal transfer with FEE-FREE tran_type (TRFOUTF). Response fee_gross=0, vat_amount=0; no FEETRF leg is posted.

Sync-commit withdraw + insert WLT_WITHDRAW_TRACK (SUBMITTED). Pre-request generates a unique `last_ext_payout_ref` env var that downstream Treasury endpoints reuse.

Fee: 0.1% rate clamped to MIN=11,000 (so withdraw of 200K → 11,000 fee gross, VAT 1,000).

exec: // Generate ext_payout_ref ONCE per run; downstream Treasury endpoints reuse it.

request.description: Merchant settlement withdraw (+ hot-shard sweep when auto_sweep). Requires a seeded merchant group — set {{group_id}} (e.g. run deploy/loadtest/setup.sql or db/tests/wallet_merchant_flow_test.sql). Tolerant of 404/400 when no group is seeded. Captures {{last_merchant_withdraw_reference}} for reversal.
Route an inbound merchant deposit/payment into a group (US-1.11): settlement while cold, a reference-hashed shard once hot. Requires a seeded merchant group — set {{group_id}}.

Account statement: ledger entries on {{acct_no_a}}, newest-first. Filter by account + optional post_date range (from/to, YYYY-MM-DD). Paged at 200 items/page — keyset via next_cursor → ?before_seq=. Captures a tran_key for the detail request.

exec: // default a 31-day statement window so the from/to demo values aren't stale
pm.test('next_cursor only on a full page', ()=>{ if (b.data.count===b.data.page_size) { pm.expect(b.data.next_cursor).to.be.a('number'); } else { pm.expect(b.data.next_cursor==null).to.be.true; } });

request.description: All legs of one transaction by tran_internal_key (run the list request first to populate {{tran_key}}).
Standalone fee + VAT charge (US-2.8). amount is the gross (VAT-inclusive) figure. Captures {{last_fee_charge_reference}} for the reversal request.
Reverse a standalone fee charge by its original reference (US-2.8). Run fee-charge first to populate {{last_fee_charge_reference}}.
Reverse an in-book transfer by its ORIGINAL reference (captured by the transfer request into {{last_transfer_reference}}). Run the transfer request first.
Reverse a topup by its ORIGINAL reference (captured into {{last_topup_reference}}). Run the topup request first.

Reverse a merchant-settlement withdraw by its original reference. Credits principal + fee/VAT back to the settlement account and emits wallet.merchant_withdraw.reversed.v1. Run merchant-withdraw first to populate {{last_merchant_withdraw_reference}}. Requires wallet.finance.reverse RBAC role (US-9.10).
Add a DEBIT hold (locks pledged_amt into available_bal). SP validates type/purpose/conflict/amount/dates. Captures {{restraint_id}} for release.

query.description: optional keyset cursor: only restraint_id < before_seq (use next_cursor)

request.description: List all restraints on {{acct_no_a}} (statuses A/R/E), newest-first. Paged at 100/page (max 200) — keyset via next_cursor -> ?before_seq=. Read-only (replica).

exec: pm.test('next_cursor only on a full page', ()=>{ if (b.data.count===b.data.page_size) { pm.expect(b.data.next_cursor).to.be.a('number'); } else { pm.expect(b.data.next_cursor==null).to.be.true; } });

request.description: One restraint by id (run add or the list request first to populate {{restraint_id}}).
Release the restraint captured into {{restraint_id}}. reason mandatory for COURT_ORDER/TAX_LIEN.
COURT_ORDER must be type ALL → conflict.
Open a CONSUMER wallet for {{client_no}} (run create-client first). Count-limited (3/CCY). Captures {{opened_acct_no}}.
Block {{opened_acct_no}} (no debits).
Close {{opened_acct_no}} (requires balance 0 → else 422 ACCT_CLOSE_NONZERO_BAL).
Account profile (no client PII).
Customer realtime balance (§9.3.1). Masked if AML_HOLD restraint active.
Historical end-of-day snapshot from WLT_ACCT_BAL (§9.3.3). today→422 INVALID_DATE; >18m→410 GONE_ONLINE.
Ops/internal full view incl. active_restraints, version, ledger/calc (§9.3.2).
Batch balance, max 100. Missing accts returned with error=ACCT_NOT_FOUND (§9.3.4).

query.description: search term, min 6 chars — matched vs acct_no / client_no

request.description: Search accounts by acct_no/client_no substring (case-insensitive). Query must be >= 6 chars (else 400). Returns acct_no + client_no + MASKED client name.

item.name: Treasury Callbacks
item.description:
State machine: SUBMITTED → ACKED → DISBURSING → COMPLETED | FAILED → REVERSED.
Each endpoint targets a specific `ext_payout_ref`; run in order after a withdraw.

request.description: Terminal success state. Idempotent — second call returns the same status without re-emitting outbox.

item.name: POST .../reverse  (auto credit-back on Treasury failure / SLA)

request.description:
Posts RVWD + RVFEE legs, credits the customer wallet back (amount + fee + VAT). Idempotent on `ext_payout_ref`. Refuses to reverse a COMPLETED withdraw — would return 409 WD_ALREADY_COMPLETED.

**Note**: this test will fail with `WD_ALREADY_COMPLETED` if you ran `completed` first. Comment out completed or use a separate withdraw.

exec: pm.test('errorMessage mentions WD_ALREADY_COMPLETED (expected if completed ran first)',

item.name: Error scenarios
item.description: Negative tests demonstrating the error envelope + HTTP status mapping.

exec: pm.test('errorCode is a SQLSTATE or synthetic code', () => pm.expect(body.errorCode).to.be.a('string'));

request.description:
Run TWICE with the SAME `reference` (hard-coded `PM-IDEMPOT-FIXED-001`).

- 1st call → 201 POSTED, balance increases by 100.
- 2nd call → 200 **DUPLICATE**, balance unchanged, same `event_uuid` returned.

Reset by changing the hard-coded reference or wiping the test wallet.

exec: // Override the collection-level auto reference so we ALWAYS use the fixed key
pm.test('POSTED on first run, DUPLICATE on retry',
console.log('Idempotency verified — same tran_key + event_uuid returned');

request.description: One-call onboarding (US-1.1): create client + KYC (phone) + first zero-balance wallet. Captures {{client_no}}/{{acct_no_a}}. phone is a fresh valid 10-digit VN number (pre-request).

Onboard a MERCHANT (US-1.7): client_type=MER, acct_type=MERCHANT. CORP/MER MUST send extra_data.business_reg_no + legal_rep (BR-09 -> 422 ORG_FIELDS_REQUIRED otherwise). phone is a fresh valid 10-digit VN number (pre-request).
Provision a NEW cold merchant group (US-1.10): group row + settlement account in one TX. Defaults: MERCHANT group, MERCHANT-type VND settlement wallet. Captures {{group_id}}.

exec: if(pm.response.code===201){ const b=pm.response.json(); pm.test('group_id + settlement_acct_no',()=>{ pm.expect(b.data.group_id).to.be.a('string'); pm.expect(b.data.settlement_acct_no).to.be.a('string'); }); pm.environment.set('group_id', b.data.group_id); }

request.description: Promote a cold merchant group (0 shards) to hot (US-1.9). shard_count omitted -> 4 (tiers 4/8/16). One-way: re-activate -> 409 GROUP_ALREADY_ACTIVATED.
Grow an already-hot group up a tier (4→8→16) and rebalance existing shards back to settlement (US-1.12). new_shard_count must be a larger hot tier than the group's current count.

exec: if([200].includes(pm.response.code)){ const b=pm.response.json(); pm.test('new_shard_count returned',()=>pm.expect(b.data.new_shard_count).to.be.a('number')); pm.test('added_acct_nos is array',()=>pm.expect(b.data.added_acct_nos).to.be.an('array')); }
